**3. @heybeluga**  
Full text: Can agentic payments save cypto and our bags?  
Within the next four years, more than 95% of AI queries could come from other AI.  
Crypto has not priced in what happens when AI becomes its biggest customer.  
Your AI is about to become the boss of other AIs.  
Those robots won’t work for free.  
Credit cards were built for humans and companies, not autonomous software.  
Crypto wallets offer a simpler solution:  
Give an agent $20 and let it buy the data, compute and services it needs.  
The rails are already being built:  
- @coinbase created x402 for native internet payments  
- @stripe launched a protocol for agent-to-agent transactions  
- @circle is building gasless nanopayments as small as $0.000001  
- @moonpay created a virtual card that lets agents spend crypto  
AI already measures compute in tokens.  
Putting those tokens onchain could allow the market to price compute while agents pay each other directly.  
The bear case?  
Big AI companies and traditional payment networks build closed systems that cut crypto out entirely.  
But if AI agents become one of the internet’s largest economic forces, the rails underneath them could become crypto’s next real product-market fit.  
Robot Venmo might actually save our bags.  
Read the full bull case, bear case and who is racing to own the rails 👇  
Date posted: 2026-07-17 15:55:12 GMT  
Engagement: 13 likes, 2 reposts, 4 replies  

**4. @mous68881**  
Full text: An AI agent just paid two cents for an API call. No card, no login, no invoice, done in under a second. No card network was involved, because none of them can process a two-cent payment without losing money on it.  
So how did it pay? This looks like a payments question. It's really four questions stacked, and only one is about moving money. The other three are trust.  
1/ Identity. Before value moves, the other side has to know which agent it's dealing with, with a track record it can check. An agent can't solve a CAPTCHA or take an SMS code, so the human login stack is useless. On-chain identity answers it: ERC-8004, live on mainnet since January, 24k+ agents. This is Know Your Agent.  
2/ Authorization. The agent acts inside a spend policy its owner set. With Circle's Agent Stack you give it a programmable USDC wallet with a global cap, per-service limits, allowlists, time-bounded sessions. Enforced at the wallet layer, checked before every tx, not after. Give it $5/day and it can't spend the 6th. Delegate spending, not control.  
3/ Settlement. The agent hits a service, gets an HTTP 402 with a price, pays per request in #USDC over x402 (open, now under the @linuxfoundation with @circle a premier member). For tiny amounts, Circle Nanopayments settles USDC gas-free down to $0.000001 by batching off-chain. That's what makes a sub-cent payment real instead of a fee-eaten rounding error.  
4/ Record. Every payment leaves a verifiable receipt on-chain. For work with a deliverable, ERC-8183 escrow releases only when terms are met. The outcome updates the agent's ERC-8004 reputation, so the next counterpart prices trust on evidence. Identity enables the payment, the payment builds reputation, reputation strengthens the next check.  
Notice steps:  
1, 2 and 4 aren't about money.  
They're trust: who is this, what can it do, what proves it happened. Settlement is being commoditized by everyone at once. The trust layer that decides whether a machine payment is safe to accept is the part still being built.  
That's what we're building. @FirmataProtocol is @Meridian_Fi's Know Your Agent layer, wiring ERC-8004 + ERC-8183 + x402 into one flow on @arc , @BuildOnCircle L1. Let a merchant or agent accept a payment from software with the same confidence a card gives a human. Testnet today, audited before mainnet.  
The money part already works. The trust part is the whole game.  
#AIagents #AgenticCommerce #x402 #ERC8004 #Stablecoins  
Date posted: 2026-07-17 17:04:26 GMT  
Engagement: 1 like, 1 repost, 1 reply  

**5. @AlgoVoi**  
Full text: AlgoVoi Mesh  
Two independent agents, each in its own container, each holding only its own post-quantum key.  
Over the network they negotiated a chain and settled agent-to-agent. Each settlement maps into a protocol envelope, x402, AP2, A2A and MPP, and the payee recomputed and verified every one offline, no service call, then refused a tampered one.  
One identity model, one protocol-agnostic settlement primitive, four envelope mappings, each recomputable offline.  
Chain-agnostic and protocol-agnostic. Live, not a mockup.  
Mesh Enterprise is coming: federation managed agents, an unlimited licensed ecosystem.  
#AgenticPayments #x402 #AP2 #A2A #PostQuantum #AIAgents  
Date posted: 2026-07-17 16:45:00 GMT  
Engagement: 3 likes, 1 repost, 0 replies

**9. @Dpw_Jules**  
Full text: One thing I’ve been paying close attention to lately is how quickly the conversation around AI is shifting from capability to accountability.  
As more AI agents begin handling payments, content, and digital interactions, proving who stands behind them is becoming just as important as what they can do.  
That’s why I’m keeping an eye on @Concordium.  
With Protocol-Level Identity, the Agent Registry, and the Verified by Concordium Badge, Concordium enables AI agents on supported ecosystems like Ethereum and Solana to prove they’re linked to a verified human or business while preserving privacy through zero knowledge proofs.  
It’s a practical approach to building trust for the agentic economy, and I think more people are starting to recognize what Concordium has been building.  
📲 Stay updated by joining the Concordium Telegram community:
